request_summary: Review the item-first lifecycle trace concept and prototype before locking the V5 discovery proof into a PRD handoff package.
user_flow_summary: The PM should move from item identification to stage understanding, evidence review, gate confidence, and PRD handoff without opening unrelated artifacts first.
information_architecture_summary: Item summary, stage timeline, linked artifacts, segments, personas, and validation state should appear as one coherent model with aggregate stage context available separately.
interaction_model_summary: The trace view should privilege current stage and next action while keeping the full discovery chain scan-friendly and auditable.
quality_summary: The prototype is credible enough for internal PM walkthroughs, but the visual hierarchy and evidence interaction still need another pass before it reaches a reference-quality bar.

quality_dimension_reviews.rationale: The PM can follow the core path, but evidence inspection still takes more attention than it should.
The prototype behaves like a real handoff review flow rather than a narrated concept.
Current stage and next action are visible, but downstream deferred work needs a crisper distinction.
Each stage stays attached to evidence and manual validation remains visible before PRD handoff.
The structure is purposeful, but the visual rhythm is not yet strong enough to count as a world-class reference surface.
The CLI-friendly structure is strong, but more non-color status cues are still required.

quality_reference_examples: A workflow cockpit that supports real evidence review without live explanation
accessibility_considerations: Gate meaning should not rely only on color or iconography.
The stage timeline should remain readable in plain-text CLI output.
trust_considerations: Every stage label should map to concrete artifacts.
Manual validation must remain visible before any PRD handoff is marked ready.
improvement_actions: Add richer inline evidence previews so stage review feels faster and more confident.
Strengthen visual contrast between current state, passed gates, and deferred future stages.
